name: PVC — Premature Ventricular Contractions
identifyBy: Early wide bizarre QRS (>120ms) without preceding P wave
Fully compensatory pause (sinus node not reset — unlike PACs)
T wave in opposite direction to QRS (discordant — secondary repolarisation change)
May occur in bigeminy (every alternate beat), trigeminy, or couplets (2 consecutive)
Uniform morphology = unifocal; variable morphology = multifocal

measurements.pWaves: Absent in ectopic beat; sinus P may march through

management: Isolated PVCs in structurally normal heart: reassure — benign
Frequent PVCs (>10,000/day or >15% of beats): risk of PVC-induced cardiomyopathy if long-standing
Echocardiogram if frequent to exclude structural disease
Reduce triggers: caffeine, stress, electrolyte abnormalities, stimulants
Beta-blockers for symptomatic PVCs; catheter ablation for refractory cases
R-on-T PVC: early PVC landing on T wave of preceding beat — can trigger VF
pearls: Compensatory pause: PVC occurs, P fires on time (buried in PVC), next sinus beat fires on time → RR interval surrounding PVC = 2x normal RR
PVC in cardiomyopathy is a high-risk finding — LVEF should be checked
Uniform (unifocal) PVCs originate from single focus; multifocal from multiple foci — higher risk
R-on-T phenomenon: PVC in the vulnerable period (on T wave) can trigger VF
PVC bigeminy may reduce effective heart rate by 50% — can cause symptoms
differentials: Aberrant PAC (wide QRS but non-compensatory pause, different P morphology)
